topic.name: Boats and Streams
topic.description: Relative motion of boats in still water and flowing streams. Downstream and upstream speeds, time and distance on rivers. Estimated reading 20 min; practice 35 min.

theory.introduction: Boats and Streams problems combine the boat's speed in still water with the stream's current. Downstream motion adds the stream speed; upstream motion subtracts it. Competitive exams frequently test finding still-water speed, stream speed, or travel time using the relations between downstream and upstream speeds.
theory.explanation:
CONCEPT EXPLANATION

Let the speed of the boat in still water be u (km/h or m/s) and the speed of the stream be v. When the boat moves downstream (with the current), effective speed = u + v. When it moves upstream (against the current), effective speed = u − v.

From any two of downstream speed, upstream speed, u, and v, the others follow: u = (downstream + upstream) / 2 and v = (downstream − upstream) / 2. Time = Distance / Effective speed. For a round trip on the same route, total time = D/(u+v) + D/(u−v).

REAL-LIFE APPLICATIONS

Ferry scheduling, cargo transport on rivers, estimating fuel consumption against currents, and navigation in tidal waters all use these relative-speed ideas.

COMMON MISTAKES

1) Adding stream speed when moving upstream instead of subtracting.
2) Using still-water speed directly without adjusting for the stream.
3) Forgetting that average speed for a round trip is NOT the arithmetic mean of downstream and upstream speeds.
4) Mixing km/h with m/s without conversion.
5) Assuming equal times for downstream and upstream legs of equal distance.

IMPORTANT NOTES

• Downstream → add stream speed; upstream → subtract stream speed.
• u and v must both be positive; u > v for upstream travel to be possible.
• Round-trip average speed = 2D / (t_down + t_up), not (u+v+u−v)/2.
• If only times for equal distances are given, use t_down and t_up to find u and v.
• Relative speed against current always reduces effective speed.

formulas.title: Downstream speed
formulas.content: Speed downstream = Speed in still water + Speed of stream = u + v

formulas.title: Upstream speed
formulas.content: Speed upstream = Speed in still water − Speed of stream = u − v

formulas.title: Time for a journey
formulas.content: Time = Distance / Effective speed (use u + v or u − v as appropriate)

formulas.title: Round-trip time (equal distance D)
formulas.content: Total time = D/(u + v) + D/(u − v)

shortcutTips.title: Halve the sum and difference
shortcutTips.tip: Still-water speed is half the sum of downstream and upstream speeds; stream speed is half their difference.

shortcutTips.title: Round-trip trap
shortcutTips.tip: Never average downstream and upstream speeds for equal distances — use total distance divided by total time.

shortcutTips.title: Equal distance shortcut
shortcutTips.tip: If downstream and upstream times for the same distance are t1 and t2, then u/v = (t1 + t2)/(t2 − t1).

shortcutTips.title: Check upstream feasibility
shortcutTips.tip: If upstream speed is negative or zero in your setup, the boat cannot travel upstream — recheck signs.

shortcutTips.title: Convert early
shortcutTips.tip: Convert all speeds to the same unit before forming u ± v.

shortcutTips.title: Man in stream analogy
shortcutTips.tip: A swimmer or boat in a stream follows the same downstream/upstream rules as trains with wind — add or subtract the current.

solvedExamples.problem: A boat travels 24 km downstream in 2 hours and 16 km upstream in 4 hours. Find the speed of the boat in still water and the speed of the stream.
solvedExamples.solution: Still-water speed = 8 km/h; stream speed = 2 km/h.

solvedExamples.problem: A man can row 6 km/h in still water. If the stream runs at 2 km/h, how long will he take to row 20 km downstream and return?
steps: Downstream speed = 6 + 2 = 8 km/h; time downstream = 20/8 = 2.5 h.
Upstream speed = 6 − 2 = 4 km/h; time upstream = 20/4 = 5 h.

solvedExamples.problem: The speed of a boat in still water is 10 km/h. It takes 5 hours to travel 48 km downstream and return. Find the speed of the stream.
steps: Let stream speed = v km/h. Downstream = 10 + v; upstream = 10 − v.

exercise.title: General Questions
exercise.description: MCQ practice on downstream/upstream speeds, still-water speed, stream speed, and round-trip problems.

questions.question: A boat goes 30 km downstream in 3 hours and returns upstream in 5 hours. What is the speed of the stream?
questions.explanation: Downstream = 10 km/h, upstream = 6 km/h. Stream = (10−6)/2 = 2 km/h.

questions.question: A man rows at 8 km/h in still water. The stream flows at 2 km/h. What is his downstream speed?
Still-water speed is 12 km/h and stream speed is 3 km/h. What is the upstream speed?
Downstream speed is 18 km/h and upstream speed is 6 km/h. Find the speed in still water.
A boat covers 40 km downstream in 4 hours. If the stream speed is 3 km/h, find the still-water speed.
A boat takes 3 hours to travel 36 km downstream and 6 hours to return. What is the speed of the boat in still water?
questions.explanation: Downstream = 12 km/h, upstream = 6 km/h. u = (12+6)/2 = 9 km/h.

questions.question: A man can row 5 km/h in still water. With a current of 1 km/h, how long to row 12 km upstream?
The ratio of downstream to upstream speed of a boat is 5 : 3. If the stream speed is 4 km/h, find the still-water speed.
questions.explanation: Let u = still water, v = 4. (u+4)/(u−4) = 5/3 ⇒ 3u+12 = 5u−20 ⇒ u = 16 km/h.

questions.question: A boat rows 15 km downstream in 2 hours and the same distance upstream in 3 hours. Find the stream speed.
questions.explanation: Downstream = 7.5 km/h, upstream = 5 km/h. v = (7.5−5)/2 = 1.25 km/h.

questions.question: A boat's speed in still water is 15 km/h. It goes 60 km downstream and back in 8 hours. Find the stream speed.
A boat travels 28 km downstream in 2 hours and 12 km upstream in 2 hours. What is the ratio of still-water speed to stream speed?
A man rows 10 km/h in still water. He takes 1 hour more to row 24 km upstream than downstream. Find the stream speed.
Downstream speed is 20 km/h and upstream speed is 12 km/h. How long to go 32 km downstream?
A boat goes 48 km downstream in 4 hours. The return journey takes 8 hours. Find the average speed for the round trip.
questions.explanation: Total distance = 96 km, total time = 12 h. Average = 96/12 = 8 km/h.

questions.question: Speed in still water is 18 km/h. Stream is 6 km/h. Time to travel 48 km downstream and 48 km upstream?
questions.explanation: Downstream: 48/(18+6) = 2 h. Upstream: 48/(18−6) = 4 h. Total = 6 hours.

questions.question: A boat covers a certain distance downstream in 2 hours and returns in 3 hours. If the stream speed is 2 km/h, find the distance.
questions.explanation: Let D be distance, u still water. D/(u+2)=2, D/(u−2)=3. From these u=10, D=24 km.

questions.question: The speed of a boat in still water is 10 km/h. A man rows to a place 30 km away and back in 7 hours. Find the stream speed.
A boat running downstream covers 16 km in 2 hours, while upstream it covers the same distance in 4 hours. What is the speed of the stream?
questions.explanation: Down = 8 km/h, up = 4 km/h. Stream = (8−4)/2 = 2 km/h.

questions.question: A boat takes twice as long to row 20 km upstream as to row 20 km downstream. If stream speed is 3 km/h, find still-water speed.
A boat goes 21 km downstream and 9 km upstream in 3 hours. It goes 12 km downstream and 18 km upstream in 3 hours. Find still-water speed.
A man rows at 4 km/h in still water. He rows to a point 12 km away and back in 6 hours 40 minutes. Find the stream speed.
If downstream speed is 24 km/h and upstream speed is 16 km/h, what distance is covered downstream in 45 minutes?
A boat can travel with a speed of 13 km/h in still water. If the speed of the stream is 4 km/h, find the time taken to go 68 km downstream.
